column5: OCR Use
column6: Key Technique
column7: Architecture

column5: No OCR is used; it explicitly uses OCR-free thumbnails, visual fetch actions, and visual working memory.
column6: Coarse-to-fine visual agent fetches thumbnails and high-resolution page crops, trained with SFT and GRPO for evidence-seeking actions.
column7: Agentic Qwen2.5-VL policy over cached page tokens, visual fetch actions, working memory, and final answer generation.

column5: Uses OCR-derived document content for outline/search and text evidence, but is not fully OCR-dependent because it also retrieves and verifies page, image, chart, and table evidence through visual tools.
column6: Tool-augmented agent builds a document outline and routes between text, image, table, and page evidence tools.
column7: Training-free orchestration around commercial LLM/VLM APIs with retrieval/search tools instead of a learned document model.

column5: Uses extracted text that may include OCR-derived strings through the document processing/read tools, but it is not OCR-dependent because the agent also reads images, tables, and page screenshots; OCR is mainly contrasted with baselines.
column6: Supervised agent-trajectory tuning teaches an LLM to search, read, and synthesize document evidence using external parsers and multimodal readers.
column7: Tool-using LLM agent; Qwen3 agent calls MinerU/Search/Read tools and an external Qwen3-VL reader rather than embedding pages end-to-end.

column5: OCR-dependent: OCR/layout parsing supplies text and element structure used by the page navigator, localizer, answer sampler, and adjudicator agents.
column6: Multi-agent tool framework decomposes long-document QA into page navigation, element localization, answer sampling, and adjudication.
column7: Training-free agent pipeline over screenshots/crops and frontier VLMs, with OCR/layout parsing tools for evidence access.

column5: No OCR is used in the proposed method; OCR appears only in baseline comparisons while DocReact reasons over multi-page document images and multimodal evidence.
column6: ReAct-style iterative reasoning retrieves multimodal page evidence and refines the answer through tool calls.
column7: Training-free agentic RAG combining visual retrievers with GPT-4o reasoning over selected heterogeneous document pages.

column5: Uses OCR/PDF parsing for the textual retrieval branch, but is not solely OCR-dependent because it also preserves page images and uses visual retrieval plus visual extraction agents.
column6: Combines BM25 text retrieval, VisRAG visual retrieval, and fine-tuned VLM agents for visual extraction and modality fusion.
column7: Multi-agent multimodal RAG with text filter, LoRA-tuned Qwen2.5-VL visual extractor, and modal fuser.

column5: No OCR is used; VLM agents operate over visual document inputs with LLM-based judgment and answer synthesis.
column6: Specializes plan, execution, judgment, and answer agents, then optimizes collaboration with SFT and GRPO rewards.
column7: Multi-agent VLM/LLM system with VLM plan/execute agents, LLM judge/answer agents, and adaptive test-time scaling.

column5: Uses OCR/PDF parsing to build the text retrieval branch, but is not fully OCR-dependent because it runs parallel image retrieval and dedicated image agents over preserved page images.
column6: Separately retrieves text and page images, extracts critical evidence, then reconciles text/image agent answers.
column7: Training-free five-agent RAG framework with ColBERT text retrieval, ColPali visual retrieval, and LLM/VLM specialist agents.

column5: Uses extracted page text as an auxiliary cue together with page images and VLM-generated summaries, but is not OCR-dependent because retrieval and reasoning are primarily visual and memory-guided.
column6: Combines dual-cue page retrieval, VLM page summaries, memory, and iterative query refinement.
column7: Training-free agentic RAG workflow using ColQwen visual retrieval and Qwen VLM/LLM reasoning over selected/summarized pages.

column5: Uses OCR text chunks in the textual retrieval pipeline, but is not fully OCR-dependent because it also uses visual retrieval and dynamic agentic inspection over images.
column6: Dynamic iterative reasoning agents alternate retrieval, inspection, and answering over text and visual document evidence.
column7: Training-free agentic RAG combining NV-Embed/ColQwen retrieval with Qwen/LLaMA/GPT VLM reasoning loops.

column5: OCR-dependent: OCR text and layout are required inputs and are fused with image features in the TILT-style encoder-decoder.
column6: OCR-aligned text-image-layout fusion with 2D relative bias and sparse attention over very long document token grids.
column7: T5-style encoder-decoder where OCR text/layout tokens are fused with U-Net visual features at each layer for long multi-page generation.

column5: OCR-dependent: it extends OCR/text-layout document VQA backbones with global document tokens and local-global reasoning.
column6: Adds document tokens and bias adaptation so page encoders exchange global information without full quadratic attention.
column7: DocFormerv2 encoder-decoder extended with alternating page-local and document-global transformer sublayers plus optional C-Former compression.

column5: OCR-dependent: per-page OCR/text-layout representations are encoded before hierarchical page-summary reasoning.
column6: Summarizes each page into learnable [PAGE] tokens and trains them with layout-aware denoising and page prediction.
column7: Hierarchical T5 encoder-decoder combining OCR/layout tokens, DiT visual patches, per-page encoders, and decoder over page summaries.

column5: OCR-dependent: OCR word and spatial embeddings are consumed with visual features while recurrent memory carries information across pages.
column6: Carries recurrent memory tokens page by page and uses curriculum training plus page selector for long documents.
column7: T5-based hierarchical/recurrent encoder-decoder with DiT visual patches, memory cells, and decoder/page head fine-tuned on full documents.

column5: Uses OCR selectively after visual evidence localization, but it is not OCR-dependent because the model directly consumes page images and performs end-to-end locate-then-read reasoning.
column6: Chain-of-Reading trains locate-then-read reasoning with Mask-AR, supervised CoR traces, and DPO.
column7: Qwen2.5-VL-based MLLM with frozen vision tower, trainable LM/projector adapters, and structured reading/action outputs.

column5: Uses OCR only indirectly: training/evaluation discuss built-in OCR capability, OCR-oriented data, and OCR baselines, while inference relies on page images and PDF-parser interleaved text-image inputs rather than external OCR.
column6: Doc-750K fine-tuning plus multimodal data packing and Ring Attention enable native long-document MLLM reading without RAG.
column7: InternVL2-style ViT-MLP-LLM with dynamic high-resolution tiles and long-context SFT on interleaved text-image/page inputs.

column5: No OCR is used; the VLM predicts evidence pages and answers from visual document inputs.
column6: Evidence-page-guided GRPO rewards models for identifying supporting pages and answering accurately.
column7: Qwen2.5-VL policy fine-tuned by RL on multi-page visual inputs with evidence supervision and answer rewards.

column5: OCR-dependent: a lightweight OCR module provides word-box tokens that are fused with layout and visual crop features for compression and streaming inference.
column6: Compresses OCR, layout, and visual crop streams into fixed 576 tokens/page with streaming abstention for long contexts.
column7: Small VLM with SigLIP2 vision encoder, OCR and vision compressors, MLP adapter, and Qwen2.5-1.5B generator.

column5: OCR-dependent: an external OCR engine supplies OCR tokens and bounding boxes to the Document-former/LLM along with image features.
column6: Document-former bridges CLIP image features with OCR/layout/instruction embeddings while freezing the base BLIP-2/FlanT5.
column7: Parameter-efficient instruction-tuned VDU model with a trainable transformer bridge and projection FFN feeding a frozen LLM.

column5: OCR-dependent: OCR text segments and coordinates are the primary model input, with layout tokens adding spatial information.
column6: Encodes each OCR bounding box as a single layout token and trains NTLP to predict interleaved text/layout.
column7: Text-layout-only LLM adaptation using frozen LLaMA/Qwen backbones with LoRA, layout tokenizer, and layout prediction head.

column5: No external OCR is used; text-rich reading is handled internally by the high-resolution multi-image VLM.
column6: Adaptive high-resolution multi-image encoding allocates visual token budget across text-rich images and compresses with pixel shuffle.
column7: Decoder-only MLLM using SigLIP visual encoder, MLP/resampler connector, and LLaMA/Mistral LM fine-tuned on multi-image instruction data.

column5: No OCR is used; it explicitly targets OCR-free multi-page document understanding from compressed high-resolution page images.
column6: Compresses each high-resolution page into 324 visual tokens using layout-guided cross-attention after vision-to-text alignment.
column7: OCR-free multi-page MLLM with ViT-L/14, H-Reducer, High-resolution DocCompressor, MAM, and mPLUG-Owl2 LLM.

column5: Uses parsed/OCR-token text when available through the PDF parsing pipeline, but is not OCR-dependent because sparse sampling operates over interleaved text and image evidence and can select visual content.
column6: End-to-end sparse sampler selects relevant text paragraphs and diagrams before the LLM sees the document.
column7: MLLM built on IXC2-VL-4KHD with shared image encoder, BGE-M3 text encoder, and trainable multimodal evidence sampler.

column5: Uses OCR during training/data construction and is trained for OCR tasks, but it is not dependent on an external OCR engine at inference because OCR is an internal VLM capability.
column6: Reinforces a unified SigLIP visual encoder with OCR/grounding data and 16x token compression via QPN/ReSA.
column7: High-resolution LVLM with dynamic crops, QPN resampler, ReSA token compressor, detection head, grounding tokens, and Qwen2 LLM.

column5: No OCR is used; evidence retrieval and answer generation operate over page images and visual embeddings.
column6: Adaptive page retrieval with frozen Pix2Struct features and a small self-attention scorer before frozen Qwen2.5-VL generation.
column7: Two-stage retriever-generator: visual page scorer selects evidence pages, then a quantized LVLM answers from selected page images.

column5: OCR-dependent: an OCR engine extracts text chunks that drive coarse-to-fine retrieval before multimodal answer generation.
column6: Coarse-to-fine OCR chunk retrieval plus attention pooling and parameter-efficient multimodal tuning.
column7: Retriever-generator using frozen Pix2Struct visual encoder, AdapterMLP bridge, and LoRA-tuned LLaMA2 for answer generation from retrieved evidence.

column5: No OCR is used; retrieval is based on visual page embeddings with neighboring-page context fusion.
column6: Learns a context-fusion adapter that reweights previous/current/next page embeddings for more accurate visual retrieval.
column7: Plug-in retriever module over frozen VisRAG/ColQwen backbones; fused page embeddings feed downstream VLM answering.

column5: No OCR is specified; it operates over already available passages, pages, tables, and graph structure rather than an OCR pipeline.
column6: Builds a knowledge graph over passages/pages/tables and fine-tunes traversal agents to select next evidence nodes.
column7: Graph-based retriever-generator where LLM/T5/MDR agents traverse document graph, then an LLM answers from retrieved context.

column5: No OCR is used; it explicitly uses page-image retrieval and VLM generation instead of text extraction/OCR.
column6: Uses multimodal page retrieval to select a compact set of relevant pages before VLM generation.
column7: Training-free visual RAG pipeline with ColPali/ColQwen-style retrievers feeding selected page images to open VLM generators.

column5: No OCR is used in the proposed method; it uses Docling PDF parsing, hierarchical text indexes, LVLM visual descriptions, and raw visual evidence, while OCR appears only in baselines.
column6: Builds flattened page chunks plus cross-page topological summaries for multi-granularity evidence retrieval.
column7: Hierarchical multimodal RAG pipeline using PDF parsing, text embeddings, LVLM visual descriptions, summary retrieval, and LLM reranking.

column5: OCR-dependent: OCR/MinerU-derived chunks, including OCR-derived table Markdown, are part of the retrieval units used for LVLM generation.
column6: Generates answerable query nodes from multimodal chunks to create a graph that retrieves evidence by query similarity.
column7: Query-centric GraphRAG over text/image/table chunks with LVLM MDoc2Query expansion and LVLM/LLM final generation.

column5: No OCR is used in the proposed method; OCR appears in text-RAG baselines and OCR-method ablations only.
column6: Traverses a page graph with semantic and VLM-assigned logical relevance to retrieve evidence pages.
column7: Logic-aware visual RAG: ColPali page graph index plus Qwen2.5-VL retrieval engine, optionally SFT-tuned as MoLoRAG+.

column5: OCR-dependent: OCR over detected regions is part of the parsing/chunking pipeline used to construct structure-aware multimodal RAG units.
column6: Fuses document parsing, OCR, and LoRA-tuned hierarchy parsing to create structure-aware RAG chunks.
column7: Pipeline architecture: detection/OCR produces annotated layout, DSHP-LLM builds header tree, DFS grouping forms retrievable chunks.

column5: Uses OCR in the textual RAG variants, but is not OCR-dependent as a model family because it also evaluates visual OCR-free RAG variants.
column6: Adapts text and visual RAG variants with fine-tuned dense retrieval/reranking and generator fine-tuning for sparse evidence.
column7: Three-stage index-retrieve-generate system supporting OCR-text chunks for VT5/Qwen and visual Pix2Struct patch retrieval.

column5: No OCR is used; Pix2Struct-style visual representations and self-attention select the relevant page image.
column6: Freezes a Pix2Struct DocVQA encoder and trains a lightweight self-attention scorer to rank pages.
column7: Two-stage visual retriever-generator: single-page Pix2Struct answerer plus learned page relevance module for multi-page selection.

column5: Uses OCR text only as a pretraining pseudo-target/signal; inference is not OCR-dependent and operates through visual document retrieval and generation over page images.
column6: Self-supervised RCR/RCG tasks compress full page-image representations into dense retrieval tokens aligned with OCR text.
column7: Visual RAG over page images using Phi3V retriever/generator pairs, frozen vision/projector blocks, and LoRA-trained LLM layers.

column2: Yes* = not fully OCR-dependent, OCR is just a component of the overall architecture
No* = OCR not explicitly used at inference, but used during PT/IT as an explicit training step
